overview.tagline: Bergen County's Hidden Gem on the Palisades
overview.description: Fairview is a compact, densely populated borough in southeastern Bergen County, perched between the Palisades cliffs and the urban corridor of North Bergen and Cliffside Park. With a predominantly Hispanic population, Fairview offers a vibrant Latin American cultural scene, affordable housing by Bergen County standards, and close proximity to both the Hudson River waterfront and the George Washington Bridge. The borough is one of the most densely populated municipalities in New Jersey.
highlights: One of the most diverse and culturally vibrant communities in Bergen County
Affordable housing compared to surrounding Bergen County towns
Thriving Latin American dining and shopping scene on Anderson Avenue
Close proximity to the George Washington Bridge and NYC
Compact, walkable community with access to public transit

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the Borough Tax Assessor. Fairview's housing stock includes multi-family homes, apartment buildings, condominiums, and some single-family homes.
realEstate.recentTrends: Fairview offers some of the most affordable real estate in Bergen County, attracting first-time buyers and investors. Multi-family properties are common and generate rental income. Prices have risen as buyers seek alternatives to pricier neighboring communities like Cliffside Park and Edgewater.
neighborhoodNotes: Anderson Avenue is the main commercial corridor with shops, restaurants, and multi-family residential
The eastern section near Cliffside Park has apartment buildings and condos with some river views
The western border near North Bergen features more compact residential streets
Properties near the Palisade Avenue corridor benefit from proximity to Englewood and GWB access

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the Borough's contracted hauler.
details: Collection days vary by location — check with Borough Hall
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use sealed bags or covered containers not exceeding 50 lbs
Bulk item pickup available by appointment

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Recycling is collected biweekly through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (plastic, glass, cans) in blue bins
Paper and cardboard collected separately
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-soiled items
Electronics recycling events held periodically

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is supplied by Veolia Water and sewer service is managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges included in property tax or utility bills
Water quality reports available annually
Report water emergencies to Veolia or DPW

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Fairview Building Department handles construction, renovation, and zoning permits.
details: Permits required for structural, electrical, plumbing, and HVAC work
Zoning applications reviewed by the Planning or Zoning Board
Certificate of Occupancy required for new construction and changes of use
Applications submitted at Borough Hall on Anderson Avenue

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Fairview manages street parking and limited municipal lots in this densely populated borough.
details: Street parking regulated throughout the borough
Overnight parking restrictions in effect on certain streets
No double parking — enforced on Anderson Avenue
Resident parking permits may be required in some areas

additionalServices.title: Recreation Programs
additionalServices.description: The Recreation Department offers youth sports and community activities.
details: Youth soccer, basketball, and baseball leagues
Summer recreation programs for children
Community events and cultural celebrations
Borough park and playground facilities

additionalServices.title: Library Services
additionalServices.description: The Fairview Free Public Library serves the community with resources and programs.
details: Located on Anderson Avenue
Bilingual (English/Spanish) programs and collections
Children's story times and reading programs
Computer access and reference services

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Fairview enforces noise restrictions given the borough's high population density.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or federal holidays
Excessive noise from residential units (music, parties) subject to fines
Commercial noise levels regulated near residential areas

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to borough standards.
keyRules: Properties must be kept clean and free of trash accumulation
Snow and ice must be cleared from sidewalks within 12 hours of storm ending
Building facades must be maintained in safe condition
Vacant properties must be secured and maintained
Graffiti must be removed promptly by property owners

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed and controlled at all times.
keyRules: All dogs must be licensed with the Borough Clerk
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Owners must clean up after their pets
Rabies vaccination required for all dogs and cats
No dangerous or exotic animals without a permit

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Fairview enforces strict parking rules due to the borough's high density and limited street space.
keyRules: No overnight parking on certain streets
Alternate-side parking enforced for street cleaning
No double parking — strictly enforced on Anderson Avenue
Fire hydrant clearance of 10 feet required
Commercial vehicle parking restricted on residential streets

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Fairview has a large rental population and enforces habitability standards.
keyRules: Landlords must register all rental units with the Borough
Certificate of Habitability required for rental occupancy
Fire safety inspections required for multi-family dwellings
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent per NJ state law
Tenants protected under NJ Anti-Eviction Act
rentals.source: Fairview Borough Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Commercial Signage
other.summary: Fairview regulates commercial signage along Anderson Avenue and other commercial corridors.
keyRules: Sign permits required for all commercial signage
Illuminated signs must comply with brightness and hours restrictions
Temporary banner permits available for limited periods
Bilingual signage is common and permitted

publicSchools.name: Fairview Elementary School (Number 1 School)
publicSchools.notes: Main elementary school serving the borough; strong ESL programs for the diverse student population

publicSchools.name: Fairview Middle School (Lincoln School)
publicSchools.notes: Middle school with honors programs; students attend Cliffside Park High School for grades 9-12 through a send-receive agreement

privateSchools.name: St. John the Baptist School (nearby Fairview/Cliffside Park)
privateSchools.notes: Catholic school serving Fairview and neighboring communities

nearbyHigherEd: Hudson County Community College — Jersey City (8 miles)

whyMoveHere: Most affordable housing in Bergen County with easy NYC access
Vibrant Latin American culture with authentic restaurants and shops on Anderson Avenue
Close proximity to the George Washington Bridge, NJ Transit buses, and Edgewater ferry
Walkable, compact community with daily needs accessible on foot
Diverse, welcoming community with a strong neighborhood feel

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus / Car via GWB
commuteToNYC.duration: 25-45 minutes by bus; 20-45 minutes by car
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit buses run from Anderson Avenue to the Port Authority Bus Terminal. Car commute via local roads to the George Washington Bridge. The Edgewater ferry terminal is nearby for a scenic commute to Midtown Manhattan.

costOfLiving.summary: Fairview offers the most affordable cost of living in Bergen County, making it accessible for first-time buyers and immigrants establishing roots.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Near or slightly below the New Jersey average

pros: Very affordable by Bergen County standards
Excellent Latin American dining and cultural scene
Close to NYC with multiple transit options
Walkable commercial corridor on Anderson Avenue
cons: Very densely populated with limited green space
School ratings below Bergen County average
No high school — students sent to Cliffside Park
Aging housing stock in some areas

restaurants.name: El Rancho de Memo
restaurants.description: Authentic Mexican restaurant serving traditional tacos, enchiladas, mole, and other regional Mexican specialties.

restaurants.name: El Artesano Bakery
restaurants.description: Popular Latin bakery offering fresh-baked bread, pastries, tres leches cake, and Colombian and Ecuadorian specialties.

restaurants.name: Don Pepe's Restaurant
restaurants.description: Family-run Colombian restaurant known for bandeja paisa, empanadas, and generous portions of traditional comfort food.

restaurants.name: Fairview Pizza
restaurants.description: Local pizzeria serving classic New Jersey-style pizza, calzones, and Italian-American favorites to the community.

parks.name: Fairview Recreation Center
parks.description: Borough recreation facility with a playground, basketball courts, and open space for community activities.

parks.name: Veteran Memorial Park
parks.description: Small memorial park honoring local veterans with monuments and seating areas.

parks.name: Palisades Interstate Park (nearby)
parks.description: The dramatic Palisades cliffs are accessible from nearby Englewood Cliffs and Edgewater, offering hiking trails and river views.

events.name: Fairview Day
events.description: Annual community celebration with food vendors, live Latin music, carnival games, and cultural performances.

events.name: Hispanic Heritage Month Celebration
events.description: Month-long celebration of Hispanic and Latin American culture with events, performances, and food throughout the borough.

events.name: Holiday Celebration
events.description: Annual holiday event with tree lighting, music, and community gathering.

attractions.name: Anderson Avenue Shopping District
attractions.description: Fairview's vibrant main street lined with Latin American restaurants, bakeries, shops, and markets creating an authentic cultural experience.

attractions.name: Fairview Free Public Library
attractions.description: Community library with bilingual programs, children's activities, and a welcoming space for the diverse population.

shoppingAreas: Anderson Avenue — Main commercial corridor with Latin American shops, restaurants, bakeries, and services
Nearby Edgewater — River Road retail with Mitsuwa Marketplace, Whole Foods, and national chains (2 miles)
Nearby Cliffside Park — Anderson Avenue extension with additional shops and dining

government.meetingSchedule: Borough Council meets on the first and third Monday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Fairview Borough Hall, 59 Anderson Avenue, Fairview, NJ 07022

hospitals.name: Hackensack Meridian Health Palisades Medical Center
